methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $100M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $1.0B - $1.5B.

data.narrative:
Mach Industries is a Series B-stage defense and security company founded by Ethan Thornton that has raised $100M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws from a peer universe of 10 comparables—2 public (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private firms operating in the same sector and stage.

Two triangulation methods were applied. The stage-progression method uses the $100M total raised as a proxy base value and applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement, yielding $190M. The peer-median method examines six same-sector, same-stage private comparables—Vannevar Labs ($1.5B), Dream Security ($1.5B), Harmattan AI ($1.4B), Castelion ($1.0B), Defense Unicorns ($1.0B), and one additional peer—producing a median valuation of $1.2B with a range of $1.0B to $1.5B.

The two methods produce material divergence. Synthesizing both approaches yields a triangulated range of $162M (low) to $1.4B (high), with a median of $695M. The significant spread reflects tension between the stage-progression method, which anchors to capital deployed, and the peer-median method, which reflects market pricing for comparable defense-tech companies. Key uncertainties include the revenue trajectory, customer concentration, and technology differentiation relative to the peer set, all of which would narrow this range materially.
